decks.notes: This deck focuses on quickly reanimating powerful creatures, such as Griselbrand, using cards like Goryo's Vengeance and Faithful Mending. It also utilizes cards like Psychic Frog and Solitude for control and removal. The deck has a variety of ways to protect its combo pieces, including Force of Negation and Teferi, Time Raveler. Additionally, it includes cards like Ephemerate and Consign to Memory for added utility and card advantage. The deck aims to quickly assemble its combo pieces to overpower the opponent and win the game before they can react effectively.

The main idea of this UR Aggro deck is to have a fast and aggressive playstyle, using low-cost creatures like Monastery Swiftspear and Dragon's Rage Channeler to deal quick damage to the opponent. The deck also includes cards like Lightning Bolt and Lava Dart for direct damage, as well as spells like Expressive Iteration and Preordain for card selection and advantage. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent early in the game and maintain pressure throughout to secure victory.

The main concept of this Boros Ponza control deck is to disrupt the opponent's land base while maintaining control of the board through removal spells and creature abilities. By using cards like Cleansing Wildfire, Wrath of the Skies, and Field of Ruin, the deck aims to limit the opponent's resources and prevent them from developing a strong board presence. Additionally, the inclusion of Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion adds an additional creature with a powerful ability to the deck. Overall, this deck prioritizes denying the opponent resources and maintaining control of the game.

This Esper Control deck focuses on controlling the game by utilizing counterspells, removal, board wipes, and card advantage tools. The deck aims to disrupt the opponent's game plan, protect its own resources, and eventually outvalue the opponent with powerful threats. With a mix of permission spells, efficient removal, and versatile threats like Teferi, Time Raveler and Ketramose, the New Dawn, the deck can adapt to different matchups and effectively control the pace of the game until it can secure a win condition.

This deck focuses on aggressive creatures that also have the ability to be blinked or returned to the battlefield for added value. By utilizing cards like Solitude and Ephemerate, it can both control the board and protect its own creatures. The goal is to overwhelm the opponent with a mix of efficient beaters and disruptive spells, ultimately outvaluing and outpacing them. The combination of aggressive creatures with blink effects allows for versatile gameplay and strategic decision-making, making it a powerful and adaptable deck in the Modern format.

The Boros Ponza deck focuses on controlling the opponent's resources and disrupting their game plan through land destruction and removal spells. It aims to slow down the opponent's mana development and board presence while maintaining card advantage through cards like Cleansing Wildfire and Solitude. With a combination of removal spells, land destruction, and powerful creatures like Elspeth, Storm Slayer, the deck seeks to outvalue the opponent and control the game until it can secure a win condition through cards like Wrath of the Skies or Price of Freedom.

The deck focuses on controlling the game through a combination of hand disruption, removal spells, and creature manipulation. It aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy by using cards like Thoughtseize and Force of Despair, while also using removal spells like Fatal Push and Witherbloom Charm to deal with threats. The deck also utilizes cards like Necrodominance and Soul Spike to take advantage of sacrificing creatures. Overall, the key strategy is to maintain control of the game while slowly grinding out advantages and eventually overpowering the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing artifacts and sacrificing them to generate value and gain board advantage. It also aims to disrupt opponent's strategy with cards like Metallic Rebuke and Tormod's Crypt. By utilizing cards like Mox Opal and Mishra's Bauble for ramp and card draw, along with powerful threats like Arcbound Ravager and Pinnacle Emissary, the deck seeks to overwhelm opponents quickly. The inclusion of Urza's Saga and Weapons Manufacturing further enhances the deck's aggressive strategy, making it a potent force in the Modern format.

This deck revolves around assembling the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, Tower) to generate a large amount of mana to cast powerful spells. It focuses on controlling the opponent's resources using powerful Eldrazi creatures and Karn, the Great Creator, while disrupting their game plan with cards like Thought-Knot Seer and Chalice of the Void. The deck aims to overwhelm the opponent with threats and powerful spells, ultimately locking them out of the game and securing victory through sheer board dominance.

This deck focuses on disrupting the opponent's strategy through counterspells and removal spells while generating card advantage with library manipulation and graveyard recursion. It aims to control the game tempo and gradually grind out a win by out-valuing the opponent. The deck also includes some creatures with disruptive abilities to further disrupt the opponent's game plan. The mana base is optimized to ensure consistency and the ability to cast spells of both colors effectively.

This UrzaTron control deck focuses on assembling the Urza lands (Urza's Tower, Urza's Power Plant, Urza's Mine) to generate massive amounts of mana, which is used to cast powerful Eldrazi creatures like Emrakul, the Promised End and Devourer of Destiny. The deck also utilizes powerful artifacts like Karn, the Great Creator and Ugin, the Eye of the Storms to control the game and disrupt the opponent's strategy. It aims to lock down the board with cards like Glaring Fleshraker and Thought-Knot Seer, while also having access to versatile removal spells like Dismember and All is Dust.

This Boros Ponza Control deck focuses on disrupting opponent's resources and controlling the board through land destruction and removal spells, while utilizing Kaheera, the Orphanguard as a companion for additional synergy. The deck aims to slow down opponents with cards like Avengers Disassembled and Wrath of the Skies, while also utilizing Karn, the Great Creator and Galvanic Discharge for additional control elements. Field of Ruin and Cleansing Wildfire are used to disrupt opponent's mana base, allowing the deck to gain advantage and secure victory through Price of Freedom and Erode in the late game.

The main focus of this deck is to control the game by utilizing powerful Eldrazi creatures and colorless mana ramp from the UrzaTron lands. Karn, the Great Creator serves as a key piece in shutting down opponent's artifacts, while Thought-Knot Seer disrupts their hand. Ugin, the Spirit Dragon provides board control and finisher potential, while Sire of Seven Deaths and Devourer of Destiny offer additional threats. The deck aims to establish a strong board presence quickly and overwhelm the opponent with powerful creatures and disruptive spells.

This deck focuses on utilizing the synergy between Broodscale Bloodchief and various support cards to create a powerful combo that can overwhelm the opponent. By maximizing the potential of sacrificing creatures and generating colorless mana, the deck aims to quickly assemble the necessary pieces to bring out Emrakul, the Promised End and secure victory. With a mix of ramp, removal, and creature support, the deck aims to disrupt the opponent's strategy while building up its own game plan for a decisive finish.

The deck focuses on generating a large amount of mana quickly through cards like Ancient Stirrings and Urza's Saga, while also utilizing creatures like Basking Broodscale and Glaring Fleshraker to control the board. The goal is to assemble a combo involving Blade of the Bloodchief and Malevolent Rumble to deal massive amounts of damage to the opponent in a single turn. Emrakul, the Promised End provides a powerful win condition if the combo is not viable. The deck also includes disruption elements like Dismember and Ghost Quarter to handle threats from the opponent.

The deck focuses on utilizing the UrzaTron lands (Urza's Mine, Power Plant, and Tower) to generate a large amount of mana quickly, allowing for powerful spells and creatures to be cast early in the game. The deck also includes various control elements such as Karn, the Great Creator and Thought-Knot Seer to disrupt the opponent's strategy. Additionally, the deck includes Eldrazi creatures and support cards to apply pressure and close out the game quickly once the UrzaTron is assembled.
